completed_steps.summary: Classified as monolith web project with Next.js App Router, Supabase-backed server actions, and internal SQL/pricing foundations
Captured user context: maximize BMAD leverage, feed strong project context, and convert implementation work into story-driven prompts/artifacts
Technology stack documented as Next.js 16, React 19, TypeScript, Supabase, Postgres SQL, Tailwind 4, npm
Deep scan completed across app routes, shared libs, and Supabase SQL artifacts; API surface and data model summarized
Development workflow and environment requirements documented
Architecture documentation generated for the monolith app
Supporting docs written: project overview, component inventory, API contracts, data models, development guide

findings.project_classification: monolith, 1 part, Next.js web app with Supabase-backed auth/data access and pricing/schema foundations
findings.user_context: Maximize BMAD leverage by giving it strong project context and structuring work as stories rather than loose tasks; reduce back-and-forth by having Jimmy translate intent into BMAD-aligned execution artifacts.
findings.technology_stack: Next.js 16 + React 19 + TypeScript frontend shell with Supabase-backed auth/data access, SQL-first Postgres schema, Tailwind 4 installed but lightly leveraged.
findings.analysis_summary: Current product strength is in template/extras configuration, pricing logic, and multi-tenant schema design; major gaps remain in real quote flow, polished UX, and AI-assisted measurement.

resume_instructions: Continue from step 11: review generated docs, refine gaps, then finalize or move to project-context generation
